packages.notes: Taxonomy label reads 'In-memory database'; Cache's MemoryStorage is an in-memory cache layer (fits the 'transient store' alias), not a database engine.

evidence.note: Surface digest resolves this decl present on linux (no gap); source is Foundation-only under Source/Shared with no os() guards, but there is no Linux CI or README corroboration - confidence reduced.

packages.notes: In-memory SQLite is a first-class configuration: SQLiteConfiguration.memory is public and every DatabaseConfigurationFactory.sqlite overload defaults to .memory.

packages.notes: Caveat: the in-memory layer is always paired with a StorageEngine; the only purely transient store in the public surface (previewStore) is DEBUG-only. Confidence lowered accordingly — Boutique is not a standalone in-memory database.

evidence.note: var items: [Item] — items are held in and read synchronously from the Store's in-memory cache layer (dual-layer memory + disk architecture)
doc: 'persisting items to a memory cache' — memory layer is intrinsic to every Store
DEBUG-only transient Store that only holds items in memory (SwiftUI Previews); no files written to disk
